code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Premature failure (≤6 months) with functional consequences
    - Negative updates erase previous satisfaction
    - "Avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification
    - Comparative inferiority without safety hazards

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in for full function
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Material flaws affecting durability without safety risks
    - Brief functional praise without specifics or enthusiasm ("They work")

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise functional praise demonstrating real-world utility
    - 3+ practical benefits praised (durability/organization/utility)
    - Solves persistent problems through daily reliability
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Close brand comparisons with demonstrated satisfaction
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite minor imperfections
    - Practical benefits significantly outweigh stated limitations

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works as intended" + validation →5 unless functional criticism exists
    - Praise + material maintenance →5 if non-functional impact
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Organizational/space benefits →5 if solving concrete problems
    - Setup effort →5 if core function flawless post-assembly
    - Aesthetic flaws →4 only if impacting durability/function
    - Brand parity + daily use →5 even with minor differences
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "We use these towels constantly - still absorbent after 6 months!"
<Reasoning>Durability praise with heavy use validation →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "These collapsible bins organize everything! Sturdy even when overfilled"
<Reasoning>Practical organizational benefits →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Our daily pan despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Brand comparison with daily use →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Non-stick coating lasts 2 months with careful use. Great heat distribution"
<Reasoning>Material flaw impacting durability →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Jewelry hanger requires complete removal for single item access"
<Reasoning>Core functionality failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Good for seasonal decorations. Wouldn't trust for heavy tools"
<Reasoning>Contextual adequacy →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Rollers jam weekly but handle bumps when working"
<Reasoning>Persistent maintenance needs →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Love the aqua design! Holds 15lbs books securely"
<Reasoning>Aesthetic + functional combination →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Not much to say. They work!"
<Reasoning>Brief functional praise without specifics →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Thinner than premium brands but handles office snacks well"
<Reasoning>Comparative adequacy →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
artifacts_json:

  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (29 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 13
  gold 5 -> 4: 7
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 4: 1
  gold 5 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "I would have given this a five star rating had it fit the appliance it was purchased for. It didn't. With that being said, make sure you are positive of the model number of your ice tea maker before ordering and don't order based on how it "looks". H..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "An awesome light weight pan and lid. Let the confections baked and eaten."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [4, 5, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I was actually looking for smaller pie cover; really only wanting something that would cover and keep my pumpkin pie from drying out when it was in the fridge.

For that purpose this Pie holder is overkill. It's LARGE. It's tall enough for meringues ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33

Summary: 29 errors, 22 borderline out of 80 examples."}
